scenario_results.name: Workload Backup Strategy Matrix
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires workload-tier backup cadence, retention, replica, restore target, assumptions, and tenant records.

scenario_results.name: Backup And Retention Architecture
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves provider-specific vault, copy, isolation, immutability, IAM, and restore-path architecture output.

scenario_results.name: Restore-Test Runbook And Evidence Packet
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires drill scope, isolated target, success criteria, escalation path, evidence artifacts, and record updates.

scenario_results.name: Compliance-Aligned Design Evidence
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires control-aligned evidence without compliance attestation or legal interpretation.

scenario_results.name: Cost And Risk Tradeoff Recommendation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario ranks lifecycle, archive, cadence, retention, and replica options by cost, recovery risk, delay, and approval.

scenario_results.name: Restore Failure From IAM Or Key Change
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario validates ordered troubleshooting across logs, vault policy, IAM, KMS, network, and audit records.

scenario_results.name: Corrupt Backup Or Ransomware Recovery
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario limits ownership to recovery validation and evidence preservation while routing containment to incident response.

scenario_results.name: Kubernetes Stateful Workload Recovery
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario covers PVC snapshots, storage classes, etcd, operator restore limits, and Kubernetes/database handoffs.

scenario_results.name: Record Intake With Missing Required Attachment
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires intake deficiency note and missing-evidence checklist instead of approval.

scenario_results.name: Unauthorized Record Change Request
families: unauthorized or unsupported record change request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario refuses direct retention mutation and routes to authorized owner with audit-trail summary.

scenario_results.name: Retention Handoff With Legal Hold Conflict
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario escalates conflicting provider, tenant, and legal-hold records rather than synthesizing an authoritative legal answer.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting Source Or Policy Handling
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario records source precedence questions and low confidence when public and tenant sources disagree.

scenario_results.name: Application-Only Refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario rejects unrelated application schema and ORM work.

scenario_results.name: Physical Datacenter Hardware Refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario rejects pure tape-library and rack-power procurement planning.

scenario_results.name: Identity Or SOC Policy Refusal
families: request requiring more specialized adjacent lane
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario routes endpoint, SOC, and identity governance policy work outside the lane.

scenario_results.name: Security Incident Containment Boundary
families: security incident containment outside recovery scope
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario limits response to recovery evidence and clean restore considerations while routing containment.

scenario_results.name: Full Cost Optimization Boundary
families: full cost optimization outside storage scope
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario routes broad FinOps/procurement work and limits commentary to backup-specific inputs.

scenario_results.name: Tenant-Specific Adaptation Without Weakening Baseline
families: industry name materially changes the pack
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves cross-industry tenant policy and regulated-data facts change records, caveats, and escalation.

scenario_results.name: Low Confidence From Missing Systems Of Record
families: industry-specific authority or safety facts missing
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario returns to orchestrator when provider inventory, job history, policy repository, and owner map are absent.

scenario_results.name: Normal In-Scope Execution With Backup Evidence Terms
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves realistic provider terminology, artifact shapes, evidence IDs, and owner approvals.

notes: All 20 scenarios are defined, mapped in functionality-map.json, and marked passing.
The suite preserves the legacy minimum of 10 scenarios and expands coverage for records administration, research-backed source handling, output fidelity, and refusal triggers.
No unreviewed regressions are recorded for the refreshed spec pack.
